system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sindhi (code=sd, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Rajasthan, district=Bhilwara.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Sindhi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Female
- age: 47
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: No Occupation / Retired / Homemaker
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Urban
- state: Rajasthan
- district: Bhilwara
- first_language: Sindhi
- clinical_persona_summary: Ujma Bano is a 47-year-old female who is currently married and resides in Bhilwara, Rajasthan, as an urban homemaker with a middle education level. She is fluent in Sindhi and Hindi, and her lifestyle includes traditional home-cooking, occasional crochet, and community-based activities. Clinically, she presents with a pragmatic approach to health, likely influenced by her cultural background and active community engagement. Her dietary habits, including the use of fresh herbs, suggest a balanced, home-prepared diet.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

Please provide surgical assessment and management plan at your earliest convenience.

Sincerely,
[[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Kumar]]

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Hospital Billing / Invoice for clinical domain Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sindhi (code=sd, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Tuberculosis & Non-Communicable Diseases — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Gujarat, district=Bhavnagar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Sindhi.

PLACEMENT (vehicle): Put VEHICLE_REGISTRATION exactly once (parking/ambulance or motor claim line). Do not dump or repeat it.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 22
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Building Insulator, Machine
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Gujarat
- district: Bhavnagar
- first_language: Sindhi
- clinical_persona_summary: Ahmed Mustafa is a 22-year-old male building insulator with a matric/secondary education, currently married and working in an urban setting in Bhavnagar, Gujarat. He is fluent in Sindhi and Gujarati, with a stress-prone personality and occasional impulsive spending. His lifestyle includes a competitive streak and curiosity for eco-friendly materials.

Document format guide for Hospital Billing / Invoice (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton (English pivot; wrap facility/geo as tags — never plain-only):
Invoice — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Barddhaman District Hospital]] District [[DISTRICT|Purba Bardhaman]]
PIN [[PIN_CODE|713101]] Patient [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] MRN [[MRN|MRN-WB-2024-0815]]

Email [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|anjali.mondal@example.com]] PAN [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]
Landline [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0342-1234567]] Vehicle [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|WB40AB1234]] (once)
Aadhaar [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|291512345678]] Policy [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-WB-7781]] GSTIN [[TAX_ID|19ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
Receipt No. INV-2024-0815 (plain prose — NOT a TYPE). State line as plain prose (NOT [[STATE|…]]).
Line items with amounts (no entity tags on procedure names).
TYPE aliases forbidden: ADDRESS_NUMBER→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS;
RECEIPT_NUMBER→plain; STATE→plain or DISTRICT.

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PIN_CODE — ZIP / Postal Code (PIN Code) e.g. [[PIN_CODE|208001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- AADHAAR_NUMBER — Aadhaar Number e.g. [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
- INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER — Insurance Policy Number e.g. [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
- TAX_ID — Tax Identification Number (TIN/GSTIN) e.g. [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
- TELEPHONE_LANDLINE — Telephone Number (Landline) e.g. [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION — Vehicle Registration Number e.g. [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
- EMAIL_ADDRESS — Email Address e.g. [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
- RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS — Residential Address e.g. [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
- PAN_NUMBER — PAN Number e.g. [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Format examples (copy the bracket pattern; invent new synthetic values):
  [[AADHAAR_NUMBER|157815659387]]
  [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
  [[ABHA_ID|12-3456-7890-1234]]
  [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]

  [[APPOINTMENT_ID|APT-240521-01]]

  [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]

  [[CVV|123]]
  [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]

  [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
  [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]
  [[EMAIL_ADDRESS|meena.devi@example.com]]
  [[EMPLOYEE_ID|EMP-77821]]
  [[ENCOUNTER_ID|ENC-55601]]
  [[GENDER|Female]]
  [[HOSPITAL_ID|HSP-UP-014]]
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
  [[IFSC_CODE|SBIN0001234]]
  [[IMEI_NUMBER|356938035643809]]
  [[INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER|POL-PB-2024-7781]]
  [[IP_ADDRESS|103.21.244.12]]
  [[MAC_ADDRESS|00:1A:2B:3C:4D:5E]]

  [[PAN_NUMBER|ABCDE1234F]]

  [[RELIGION|Hindu]]
  [[RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS|12 Gandhi Nagar, Ward 4]]
  [[STUDENT_ID|STU-2024-0912]]
  [[TAX_ID|29ABCDE1234F1Z5]]
  [[TELEPHONE_LANDLINE|0512-2345678]]
  [[URL|https://example.org/u/meena]]
  [[VEHICLE_REGISTRATION|UP78AB1234]]
  [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
  [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
  [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]

Latin/digits OK inside these entity values even for Indic documents: AADHAAR_NUMBER, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, DRIVING_LICENCE, VOTER_ID, BPL_RATION_CARD, TAX_ID, PHONE_NUMBER, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, PIN_CODE, IP_ADDRESS, URL, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, IFSC_CODE, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, PIN, EMPLOYEE_ID, STUDENT_ID, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, ABHA_ID, ABHA_ADDRESS, MRN, PATIENT_ID, HOSPITAL_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, ENCOUNTER_ID, APPOINTMENT_ID, REFERRAL_ID, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, WARD_NUMBER
Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Discharge Summary for clinical domain General Medicine.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sindhi (code=sd, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match General Medicine — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Gujarat, district=Bhavnagar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Sindhi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 22
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Building Insulator, Machine
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Gujarat
- district: Bhavnagar
- first_language: Sindhi
- clinical_persona_summary: Ahmed Mustafa is a 22-year-old male, currently married, working as a building insulator in urban Gujarat. He has a matriculation education and is fluent in Sindhi, Gujarati, and Hindi. His stress-prone nature and impulsive spending habits may impact his health management.

Document format guide for Discharge Summary (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|...]] DOB [[DOB|1994-06-21]] [[AGE|30]] [[GENDER|Female]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|...]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]] | Course / advice: <concise paragraphs matching domain + persona sex/age>

MANDATORY inline entities (14 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOB — Date of Birth e.g. [[DOB|1992-03-14]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- ADMISSION_NUMBER — Admission Number e.g. [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0912]]
- WARD_NUMBER — Ward Number e.g. [[WARD_NUMBER|B2]]
- BED_NUMBER — Bed Number e.g. [[BED_NUMBER|12]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- ABHA_ADDRESS — ABHA Address e.g. [[ABHA_ADDRESS|meena.devi@abdm]]
- CASTE — Caste e.g. [[CASTE|OBC]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
خروج نامو — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bhavnagar Civil Hospital]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Mustafa]] پيدائش جو تاريخ [[DOB|2002-08-15]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]]
داخلو [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0728]] وارڊ [[WARD_NUMBER|A3]] بسترو [[BED_NUMBER|07]]
ڊاڪٽر [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]] | علاج جو طريقو / صلاح:
مريض کي کاڌي جي زهر سبب اڪيٽ گيسٽرو اينٽرائٽس داخل ڪيو ويو. مريض ۾ اُلٽيون، ميداني ڪنب، ۽ پاڻي جي کوٽ جون علامتون پيش آيون.
مريض جو علاج IV fluids، antiemetics، ۽ antibiotics سان ڪيو ويو. مريض جو علاج سٺو جواب ڏنو، ۽ هاڻي afebrile آهي نارمل بسترن سان.
مريض کي صلاح ڏني وئي آهي پاڻي پيئڻ، نرم خوراڪ کائڻ، ۽ هڪ هفتي اندر gastroenterologist سان فالو اپ ڪرڻ.
مريض کي خوراڪ جي حفاظت ۽ صفائيءَ جا اصول سمجھايا ويا.

[[RELATIVE_NAME|Amina Mustafa]]
[[DISTRICT|Bhavnagar]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|ahmed.mustafa@abdm]]
[[CASTE|Muslim]]
generated_text_en:
Discharge Summary — [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bhavnagar Civil Hospital]]
[[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Mustafa]] DOB [[DOB|2002-08-15]] [[AGE|22]] [[GENDER|Male]]
Adm [[ADMISSION_NUMBER|ADM-2024-0728]] Ward [[WARD_NUMBER|A3]] Bed [[BED_NUMBER|07]]
Dr [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]] | Course / advice: Patient admitted with acute gastroenteritis secondary to food poisoning. Presented with vomiting, abdominal cramps, and dehydration. Managed with IV fluids, antiemetics, and antibiotics. Patient responded well to treatment and is now afebrile with normalized bowel movements. Advised to maintain hydration, consume light diet, and follow up with gastroenterologist in one week. Patient counseled on food safety and hygiene practices.

[[RELATIVE_NAME|Amina Mustafa]]
[[DISTRICT|Bhavnagar]]
[[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]]
[[ABHA_ADDRESS|ahmed.mustafa@abdm]]
[[CASTE|Muslim]]
judge_reasoning: Sindhi prose in Arabic script; all entity types allowed; no persona/clinical mismatch; plausible content and length.

professional_persona: Ahmed Mustafa, a 22-year-old insulation specialist adept at operating thermal spray machines and measuring thermal conductivity, blends his methodical, organized workflow with a curiosity for eco-friendly materials, while his talkative, sociable demeanor helps him liaise with clients and crew, and his competitive, skeptical mindset drives him to constantly benchmark his performance despite occasional stress.
linguistic_persona: Ahmed Mustafa speaks Sindhi fluently, is proficient in Gujarati for daily work and community interaction, communicates effectively in Hindi across broader contexts, and reads technical English manuals well enough to follow safety guidelines and equipment instructions.
sports_persona: Ahmed Mustafa, a devoted cricket enthusiast, plays informal matches at the local ground on weekends, follows the Gujarat Titans in the IPL, and regularly debates match tactics with friends, using his competitive streak to sharpen his batting and fielding skills.
arts_persona: Ahmed Mustafa enjoys listening to traditional Sindhi folk music, often singing songs like ‘Ho Jamalo’, appreciates Bollywood classics starring Shah Rukh Khan, and attends local cultural festivals to experience folk dance performances, reflecting his love for vibrant artistic expression.
travel_persona: Ahmed Mustafa loves weekend getaways to places like Gir Forest to spot lions, enjoys exploring the historic forts of Rajasthan during spring breaks, and dreams of a future pilgrimage to Istanbul to experience its architectural marvels, planning trips through budget travel apps and sharing rides with friends.
culinary_persona: Ahmed Mustafa enjoys cooking and savoring Sindhi delicacies such as sai bhaji, kheer, and Sindhi biryani, often experimenting with fresh coriander and mustard seeds, while also appreciating Gujarati snacks like dhokla and enjoying weekend brunches at local cafés.
persona: Ahmed Mustafa is a talkative, detail-oriented 22-year-old insulation technician whose competitive edge and curiosity drive him to master eco-friendly materials, even as his stress-prone nature and occasional impulsive spending add a human touch to his disciplined routine.
cultural_background: Ahmed comes from a Sindhi Muslim family that has been living in Bhavnagar, Gujarat for several generations. The family blends traditional Sindhi customs—such as the celebration of Cheti Chand, love for Sindhi cuisine, and community gatherings at the local masjid—with the vibrant urban lifestyle of Gujarat, where markets, festivals, and a spirit of entrepreneurship are commonplace. Growing up in an urban setting, he was exposed to both the close‑knit family values that emphasize respect for elders and hospitality, and the pragmatic, results‑oriented mindset prevalent among Gujarat’s middle‑class working families.
linguistic_background: Ahmed is fluent in Sindhi, his mother tongue, and can read, write and speak it with ease. He is also proficient in Gujarati, the regional language of Gujarat, which he uses daily at work and in the community. His third language, Hindi, is spoken and understood well, allowing him to communicate across wider Indian contexts. Having completed secondary school, he has functional literacy in all three languages and possesses basic English reading skills sufficient for interpreting technical manuals and safety guidelines.
skills_and_expertise: Ahmed has built practical expertise in building insulation, specializing in the installation of thermal insulation materials in both residential and commercial structures using mechanised equipment. He is adept at operating insulation spraying machines, measuring thermal conductivity, ensuring compliance with safety standards, and performing basic troubleshooting on the machinery. His secondary skills include project planning, material handling and inventory management, basic carpentry for on‑site modifications, and effective customer communication. His methodical approach and attention to detail make him a reliable technician on construction sites.
skills_and_expertise_list: ["Building insulation installation", "Operation of insulation spraying machines", "Thermal conductivity measurement", "Safety compliance and hazard assessment", "Technical troubleshooting of equipment", "Project planning and scheduling", "Material handling and inventory management", "Customer interaction and negotiation", "Basic carpentry and repair", "Workplace ergonomics and stress management"]
hobbies_and_interests: Ahmed enjoys socialising with friends and family, often organizing gatherings at his home. He is an avid cricket fan and participates in informal matches at local grounds. He loves listening to Sindhi folk music and occasionally sings traditional songs. His curiosity drives him to read technical construction magazines and stay updated on new insulation materials. In his leisure time, he watches Bollywood movies and experiments with cooking Sindhi dishes such as sai bhaji and kheer. He also takes part in community events and religious celebrations.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Social gatherings with friends and family", "Playing cricket", "Listening to Sindhi folk music", "Reading technical construction magazines", "Watching Bollywood movies", "Cooking Sindhi dishes (sai bhaji, kheer)", "Participating in community and religious events"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Ahmed aims to become a senior insulation specialist and eventually lead a team of technicians in Gujarat. He plans to obtain certification in advanced thermal insulation technologies and stay updated with eco‑friendly materials. Over the next five years, he envisions launching his own small insulation contracting business to serve residential and commercial clients, providing stable income for his family. Recognising his tendency toward stress, he also intends to undergo formal stress‑management training to improve his resilience and maintain high performance on demanding projects.
persona_clinical_summary: Ahmed Mustafa is a 22-year-old male building insulator with a matriculation education, currently married and residing in Bhavnagar, Gujarat. He is fluent in Sindhi and Gujarati, works as a machine operator, and reports stress-prone behavior with occasional impulsive spending. His lifestyle includes a competitive streak and curiosity about eco-friendly materials.

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete Referral Letter for clinical domain Oncology / Complex Chronic Care.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Sindhi (code=sd, script=Arabic). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Oncology / Complex Chronic Care — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Gujarat, district=Bhavnagar.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Arabic for Sindhi.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 22
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Building Insulator, Machine
- education_level: Matric/Secondary
- zone: Urban
- state: Gujarat
- district: Bhavnagar
- first_language: Sindhi
- clinical_persona_summary: Ahmed Mustafa is a 22-year-old male building insulator with a matriculation education, currently married and residing in Bhavnagar, Gujarat. He is fluent in Sindhi and Gujarati, works as a machine operator, and reports stress-prone behavior with occasional impulsive spending. His lifestyle includes a competitive streak and curiosity about eco-friendly materials.

Document format guide for Referral Letter (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-01]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|...]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|...]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|36]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Reason: <domain-appropriate clinical reason matching persona>

MANDATORY inline entities (12 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- DOCTOR_NAME — Doctor Name e.g. [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Anjali Sharma]]
- HOSPITAL_NAME — Hospital / Institution Name e.g. [[HOSPITAL_NAME|District Hospital Kanpur]]
- REFERRAL_ID — Referral ID e.g. [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-1123]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- MRN — Medical Record Number (MRN) e.g. [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- PASSPORT_NUMBER — Passport Number e.g. [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|Z1234567]]
- DRIVING_LICENCE — Driving Licence Number e.g. [[DRIVING_LICENCE|UP14 20110012345]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.
generated_text:
خطو [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815]] کان [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bhavnagar Civil Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]
بابت [[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Mustafa]], [[AGE|22]] / [[GENDER|Male]], ضلعو [[DISTRICT|Bhavnagar]] سبب: مشڪوڪ کاٻي بغل جو ڳوڙو سان جسماني علامتون چڪاس

عزيز آنڪولوجي جو شعبو،

اسان رفر ڪري رهيا آهيون [[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Mustafa]], 22 سالن جو [[GENDER|Male]] تعميراتي انسوليٽر، تڪليف واري عادت ۽ اچتو خرچ جي تاريخ سان، مخصُص آنڪولوجي چڪاس لاء. مريضو گذريل 6 هفتن کان وڌندڙ کاٻي بغل جو ڳوڙو پيش ڪري ٿو، مدھم سور سان گڏ رات جو پسو. هو گذريل مهيني تقريبًا 4 ڪلوگرام اچتو وزن گھٽجڻ ۽ مستقل تھڪاوٽ، جيڪا مشين چلائڻ وارو طور سندس ڪم جي ڪارڪردگي متاثر ڪري ٿي، بابت ٻڌائي ٿو.

چڪاس تي، 3x2 cm سخت، نہ چلون وارو ڳوڙو کاٻي بغل ۾ مدھم سور سان محسوس ٿي سگھي ٿو. ڪو گردن جي لمف نوڊس نوٽ ڪيو ويو ناهي. ليبارٽري چڪاس مائل انيميا، 10.2 g/dL هيموگلوبن سان، ۽ 45 mm/hr وڌل ESR ظاھر ڪن ٿا. ابتدائي الٽراساؤنڊ گائيڊڊ فائن نيدل ايسپائريشن اٽپيڪل لمفائيڊ سيلز، لمفوما جو اشارو، ڏيکاري ٿو.

مريضو جا [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] رڪارڊ اڳوڻو ڪو ڪينسر ناهي ڏيکارن. سندس [[RELATIVE_NAME|Mustafa Ahmed]] (پيٽر) بلڊ پريشر جي تاريخ سان آهي. مريضو فالو اپ ڪارڊينيشن لاء [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] تائين پهچي سگھجي ٿو.

مهرباني ڪري مڪمل آنڪولوجي چڪاس جو انتظام ڪيو، جنهن ۾ PET-CT اسڪين ۽ هڏن جي ماڙ چڪاس شامل هجي. مريضو جا [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P1234567]] ۽ [[DRIVING_LICENCE|GJ01 20200012345]] سڃاڻپ لاء دستياب آهن.

مخلصاڻي طرح،
ڊاڪٽر راجيش پٽيل
جنرل ميڊيسن جو شعبو
ڀاوب نگر سول اسپتال
[[DISTRICT|Bhavnagar]], Gujarat
generated_text_en:
Referral [[REFERRAL_ID|REF-2024-0815]] from [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Bhavnagar Civil Hospital]] / [[DOCTOR_NAME|Dr. Rajesh Patel]]
Re: [[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Mustafa]], [[AGE|22]] / [[GENDER|Male]], District [[DISTRICT|Bhavnagar]]
Reason: Evaluation of suspicious left axillary mass with constitutional symptoms

Dear Oncology Department,

This is to refer [[PATIENT_NAME|Ahmed Mustafa]], a 22-year-old male building insulator with a history of stress-prone behavior and occasional impulsive spending, for specialized oncological evaluation. The patient presents with a progressively enlarging left axillary mass over the past 6 weeks, associated with intermittent dull pain and night sweats. He reports unintentional weight loss of approximately 4 kg in the last month and persistent fatigue affecting his work performance as a machine operator.

On examination, a 3x2 cm firm, non-mobile mass is palpable in the left axilla with mild tenderness. No cervical lymphadenopathy is noted. Laboratory investigations reveal mild anemia with hemoglobin of 10.2 g/dL and elevated ESR of 45 mm/hr. Initial ultrasound-guided fine needle aspiration shows atypical lymphoid cells suggestive of lymphoma.

The patient's [[MRN|MRN-2024-0815-001]] records show no prior malignancy. His [[RELATIVE_NAME|Mustafa Ahmed]] (father) has a history of hypertension. The patient can be reached at [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876543210]] for follow-up coordination.

Please arrange for comprehensive oncological workup including PET-CT scan and bone marrow biopsy. The patient's [[PASSPORT_NUMBER|P1234567]] and [[DRIVING_LICENCE|GJ01 20200012345]] are available for identification purposes.

Sincerely,
Dr. Rajesh Patel
Department of General Medicine
Bhavnagar Civil Hospital
[[DISTRICT|Bhavnagar]], [[STATE|Gujarat]]

persona: Krishnan Kumar blends a methodical work ethic with a love for folk art, balancing disciplined loom management with spontaneous rangoli creations and weekend cricket, all while juggling a modest saving habit and a lingering habit of postponing routine health check‑ups.
cultural_background: Krishnan comes from a middle‑class Tamil family living in the rural outskirts of Erode, Tamil Nadu, a town renowned for its textile industry and weaving traditions. His upbringing was steeped in Tamil cultural practices—celebrating Pongal, attending bhakti music evenings at the local Shiva temple, and helping his family with textile work in the loom shed. The close‑knit village community, with its cooperative spirit and practical outlook, has nurtured his organized, reliable nature while his curiosity is fed by the vibrant folk art and colourful sari designs he encounters daily.
linguistic_background: Krishnan’s mother tongue is Tamil. He reads and writes Tamil fluently, using the Tamil script both at home and in his work records. He completed primary school in a government school where English was taught as a second language; consequently, he can understand spoken English and manage basic workplace communication, though he seldom writes formal English documents. He has limited exposure to Hindi—enough to comprehend simple spoken instructions and read basic signs, but he does not converse in Hindi regularly.
skills_and_expertise: Krishnan possesses hands‑on expertise in the operation and maintenance of power looms and other textile machinery. He is adept at quality inspection, ensuring that woven fabrics meet prescribed standards for strength, colourfastness, and pattern accuracy. His experience as a textile jobber includes inventory management of yarns and finished goods, basic bookkeeping for daily wages, and coordinating shift schedules for a small team. His organized approach enables him to plan production batches, track material usage, and troubleshoot minor mechanical issues promptly. Additionally, his curiosity about art has led him to develop an eye for design details, assisting senior supervisors in selecting colour palettes and fabric finishes.

hobbies_and_interests: Krishnan enjoys attending local folk music concerts, especially those featuring nadaswaram and thappu drumming. He is an avid fan of Kollywood movies and follows contemporary Tamil cinema trends. In his free time, he likes to experiment with traditional rangoli designs using natural pigments, and he occasionally helps his wife in cooking classic Tamil dishes like sambar and idli. His curiosity drives him to read articles about textile innovations and he often visits nearby pottery workshops to learn new crafts. He also participates in community cricket matches and enjoys trekking short trails around the outskirts of Erode.
hobbies_and_interests_list: ["Folk Music Concerts", "Kollywood Movies", "Traditional Rangoli Art", "Cooking Tamil Cuisine", "Reading Textile Innovation Articles", "Visiting Pottery Workshops", "Community Cricket", "Trekking Around Erode"]
career_goals_and_ambitions: Krishnan aims to advance from his current jobber role to a supervisory position within the textile unit. He plans to enrol in a short‑term diploma in Textile Technology to upgrade his technical knowledge and acquire skills in modern loom automation and sustainable fabric production. In the next five years, he envisions leading a small cooperative workshop that blends traditional Tamil weaving techniques with contemporary designs, thereby creating market‑ready products for both domestic and export markets. He also aspires to improve his English proficiency to handle client communications and eventually take on a managerial role that involves strategic planning and workforce development.

system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

Do NOT invent types like DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER. Put calendar dates/times in plain prose.

Quality bar (BAD vs GOOD — avoid known judge/auditor failures):
Aim for PERFECT documents. Avoid these known failure modes:

BAD → dialect_script_impurity:
  English (or wrong-script) clinical body when Gujarati/Tamil/Meitei/Ol Chiki/Arabic/… is required.
GOOD:
  Full clinical prose in the assigned language AND exact script. Latin digits only inside ID tags
  (Aadhaar, phone, MRN, IFSC, email). Drug/lab names may stay Latin.

BAD → instruction_drift:
  Assigned domain is oncology but the note narrates generic TB / unrelated complaint.
GOOD:
  Chief complaint, findings, and plan clearly match the assigned clinical domain.

BAD → surrogate_plausibility_collapse:
  Persona district=Faridkot but VILLAGE/HOSPITAL in another state; ABHA local-part unrelated to PATIENT_NAME.
GOOD:
  All places grounded in persona state+district; names and ABHA coherent with PATIENT_NAME.

BAD → domain_persona_mismatch:
  Male persona with maternal delivery; age 70 with paediatric content;
  geriatric / extreme-age routine 28-week pregnancy.
GOOD:
  Sex/age in narrative and tags match persona exactly; maternal content is
  age-plausible (no unsafe extreme late pregnancy as a routine story).

BAD → missing tags / HOSPITAL_ID only:
  Facility name in prose, only [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]] tagged; doctor name untagged.
GOOD:
  [[HOSPITAL_NAME|…]] AND [[HOSPITAL_ID|…]]; [[DOCTOR_NAME|…]] whenever a doctor is mentioned.

BAD → entity stuffing / long SMS:
  AGE/PHONE/EMAIL repeated every chat turn; SMS that is a full discharge dump.
GOOD:
  Each TYPE once (twice max); SMS ≤ ~400 chars with every mandatory tag once.

BAD → invented types / bad checksums:
  [[DATE|…]], [[POLICY_NUMBER|…]]; Aadhaar failing Verhoeff; toy phones like 1234567890.
GOOD:
  Only allow-listed TYPEs (billing: EMAIL→EMAIL_ADDRESS, ADDRESS→RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS;
  receipt/state/money stay plain prose). Valid-looking synthetic Aadhaar/phone.

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.
user_prompt:
Write one complete ASHA Worker Note for clinical domain Surgical.

GENERATION LANGUAGE: English (Latin script) — this is an English pivot draft. A later pipeline stage will translate clinical prose into Tamil (code=ta, script=Tamil). Keep [[TYPE|value]] tags intact and valid.
CRITICAL: TYPE names are NEVER translated or localized — always exact ASCII allow-list (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). Forbidden: [[রোগীর_নাম|…]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]].
ID-like tagged values stay Latin/digits. Drug names / labs stay untagged.
DOMAIN ANCHOR: chief complaint, findings, and plan MUST match Surgical — do not default to unrelated TB/generic content unless that is the assigned domain.
GEO ANCHOR: any DISTRICT/VILLAGE/HOSPITAL_NAME must be plausible for persona state=Tamil Nadu, district=Erode.
NOTE: translation stage (not this draft) will put the body into Tamil for Tamil.

Persona anchors (must remain consistent; patient = this persona):
- sex / gender: Male
- age: 25
- marital_status: Currently Married
- occupation: Jobber (Textile)
- education_level: Middle
- zone: Rural
- state: Tamil Nadu
- district: Erode
- first_language: Tamil
- clinical_persona_summary: A 25-year-old male from rural Tamil Nadu, currently married, works as a textile jobber with middle education. He speaks Tamil and has a modest saving habit, with a lifestyle that includes weekend cricket and a preference for local folk concerts. His work is methodical, and he resides in the Erode district.

Document format guide for ASHA Worker Note (English structure; a later stage translates to the target language):
Example skeleton:
ASHA note — Village [[VILLAGE|...]], District [[DISTRICT|...]]
Beneficiary [[PATIENT_NAME|...]], [[AGE|26]] / [[GENDER|Female]]
ASHA: [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|...]] | Phone [[PHONE_NUMBER|98XXXXXXXX]]
Visit findings: <2-4 short sentences>

MANDATORY inline entities (11 types) — EACH must appear ≥ once as [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]. Missing ANY is a hard failure:
- PATIENT_NAME — Patient Name e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Meena Devi]]
- AGE — Age e.g. [[AGE|34]]
- GENDER — Gender e.g. [[GENDER|Female]]
- ASHA_WORKER_NAME — ASHA Worker Name e.g. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Sunita Yadav]]
- VILLAGE — Village e.g. [[VILLAGE|Shivpur]]
- DISTRICT — District e.g. [[DISTRICT|Kanpur Nagar]]
- PHONE_NUMBER — Phone Number e.g. [[PHONE_NUMBER|9876512340]]
- RELATIVE_NAME — Relative Name (Father/Mother/Spouse/Guardian) e.g. [[RELATIVE_NAME|Ramesh Devi]]
- BPL_RATION_CARD — BPL / Ration Card Number e.g. [[BPL_RATION_CARD|BPL-UP-208001-4421]]
- VOTER_ID — Voter ID e.g. [[VOTER_ID|ABC1234567]]
- RELIGION — Religion e.g. [[RELIGION|Hindu]]

Before finishing, mentally checklist every mandatory TYPE above.
Do NOT invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, TOTAL_AMOUNT, GST_AMOUNT, GRAND_TOTAL). Put dates/times and money amounts in plain prose.
Produce ONLY the clinical document text with inline annotations. No markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

persona: Krishnan Kumar blends a methodical work ethic with a love for folk art, balancing disciplined loom management with spontaneous rangoli creations and weekend cricket, all while juggling a modest saving habit and a lingering habit of postponing routine health check‑ups.
cultural_background: Krishnan comes from a middle‑class Tamil family living in the rural outskirts of Erode, Tamil Nadu, a town renowned for its textile industry and weaving traditions. His upbringing was steeped in Tamil cultural practices—celebrating Pongal, attending bhakti music evenings at the local Shiva temple, and helping his family with textile work in the loom shed. The close‑knit village community, with its cooperative spirit and practical outlook, has nurtured his organized, reliable nature while his curiosity is fed by the vibrant folk art and colourful sari designs he encounters daily.
linguistic_background: Krishnan’s mother tongue is Tamil. He reads and writes Tamil fluently, using the Tamil script both at home and in his work records. He completed primary school in a government school where English was taught as a second language; consequently, he can understand spoken English and manage basic workplace communication, though he seldom writes formal English documents. He has limited exposure to Hindi—enough to comprehend simple spoken instructions and read basic signs, but he does not converse in Hindi regularly.
skills_and_expertise: Krishnan possesses hands‑on expertise in the operation and maintenance of power looms and other textile machinery. He is adept at quality inspection, ensuring that woven fabrics meet prescribed standards for strength, colourfastness, and pattern accuracy. His experience as a textile jobber includes inventory management of yarns and finished goods, basic bookkeeping for daily wages, and coordinating shift schedules for a small team. His organized approach enables him to plan production batches, track material usage, and troubleshoot minor mechanical issues promptly. Additionally, his curiosity about art has led him to develop an eye for design details, assisting senior supervisors in selecting colour palettes and fabric finishes.

career_goals_and_ambitions: Krishnan aims to advance from his current jobber role to a supervisory position within the textile unit. system_prompt:
You generate synthetic Indian clinical documents with inline surrogate PHI/PII annotations for dataset creation under DPDP-safe research use.

GENERATION STAGE = ENGLISH PIVOT: write clinical prose in English (Latin script). A later pipeline stage translates prose into the target Indic language/script. Do NOT write the document body in Bengali/Hindi/Tamil/etc. at this stage.

CRITICAL — TYPE names are NEVER translated: always exact ASCII uppercase allow-list ids, e.g. [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]] NOT [[রোগীর_নাম|…]] or [[रोगी_नाम|…]] or [[PatientName|…]].

Inline annotation format: [[TYPE|surrogate_value]]
Every PII/PHI mention in the generated clinical text MUST be wrapped as [[ENTITY_TYPE|surrogate_value]] using an entity id from the allow-list only. Do not emit unlabeled real-looking identifiers. Do not invent TYPE names (no DATE, TIME, POLICY_NUMBER, MEDICAL_RECORD_NUMBER — use MRN / APPOINTMENT_ID / etc.).

Rules:
- Use ONLY synthetic surrogate values. Never copy real people or real IDs.
- Keep the same surrogate for the same entity type within one document (DICS).
- At generation (English pivot): write clinical prose in English. TYPE names stay exact ASCII allow-list forever (PATIENT_NAME, MRN, …). NEVER translate or localize the TYPE token itself. A later stage translates prose to the target Indic script.

- ID-like entity values (Aadhaar, PAN, IFSC, MRN, phone, policy numbers, emails) MAY stay Latin/digits even in Indic documents — that is expected, not impurity.

- Use ONLY allow-list TYPE names. Billing aliases: EMAIL/CONTACT_EMAIL → EMAIL_ADDRESS; ADDRESS_NUMBER → RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS; RECEIPT_NUMBER and STATE are NOT types (receipt no. / state stay plain prose; geography uses DISTRICT).

- PAN must match ^[A-Z]{5}[0-9]{4}[A-Z]$ e.g. ABCDE1234F — not KAAPR1234C-style junk.

- Person/place names should prefer the document script when natural; Latin names are OK if culturally plausible.
- Ground geography in the persona state/district/zone when location appears.
- Age and gender in the note MUST match the persona fields exactly.
- Clinical content MUST fit the assigned domain AND the persona (no maternal delivery for male patients; no paediatric domain for adults).
- Maternal_health must be age-plausible: routine ANC/delivery for ages ~18–40; ages 40–42 → high-risk ANC / PNC / contraception — never invent extreme late pregnancy (e.g. 49yo as routine 28-week gestation).

- Include every mandatory entity from the doc-type profile at least once (required ∪ optional when require_all_profile_entities is on).

- Prefer exactly one tag per mandatory TYPE. Never dump/repeat the same TYPE to pad coverage (max 2 occurrences). No trailing entity laundry-lists.

- Device/network IDs (IP_ADDRESS, URL, IMEI_NUMBER, MAC_ADDRESS) belong only in telemedicine session metadata — once each, never inside chat turns.

- VEHICLE_REGISTRATION belongs only in ER arrival, hospital billing, or insurance claim lines — once, never dumped.

- Optional entities may be included when natural for the document type.
- Do not invent entity TYPE names outside the provided allow-list.
- Never invent DATE, TIME, APPOINTMENT_DATE, APPOINTMENT_TIME, POLICY_NUMBER — write calendar dates/times as plain prose outside tags.

- Do not wrap non-PHI clinical prose (symptoms, meds, vitals, drug names) as entities.
- Never tag currency/amounts (rupees, GST, totals, line-item rates) — leave as plain numbers.
- Use 10-digit Indian mobiles (optional +91 / 91 prefix). Avoid toy patterns.
- Aadhaar must be 12 digits, leading 2–9, Verhoeff-valid (synthetic checksum OK).
- automated_sms must stay short (≤ ~400 characters, 2–4 sentences) — never dump a full chart into an SMS.
- Output ONLY the clinical document text — no markdown fences, no preamble, no postscript.

ENTITY TYPE ALLOW-LIST (use ONLY these TYPE names — copy spelling exactly):
AADHAAR_NUMBER, ABHA_ADDRESS, ABHA_ID, ADMISSION_NUMBER, AGE, APPOINTMENT_ID, ASHA_WORKER_NAME, BANK_ACCOUNT_NUMBER, BANK_ROUTING_NUMBER, BED_NUMBER, BPL_RATION_CARD, CASTE, CREDIT_CARD_NUMBER, CVV, DISTRICT, DOB, DOCTOR_NAME, DRIVING_LICENCE, EMAIL_ADDRESS, EMPLOYEE_ID, ENCOUNTER_ID, GENDER, HOSPITAL_ID, HOSPITAL_NAME, IFSC_CODE, IMEI_NUMBER, INSURANCE_POLICY_NUMBER, IP_ADDRESS, MAC_ADDRESS, MRN, OCCUPATION, PAN_NUMBER, PASSPORT_NUMBER, PATIENT_ID, PATIENT_NAME, PHONE_NUMBER, PIN, PIN_CODE, REFERRAL_ID, RELATIVE_NAME, RELIGION, RESIDENTIAL_ADDRESS, STUDENT_ID, TAX_ID, TELEPHONE_LANDLINE, URL, VEHICLE_REGISTRATION, VILLAGE, VOTER_ID, WARD_NUMBER

BAD → localized / translated TYPE names (kills tag protection):
  [[রোগীর_নাম|Anjali]], [[रोगी_नाम|…]], [[PatientName|…]], [[एम.आर.एन|…]].
GOOD:
  Exact ASCII allow-list only: [[PATIENT_NAME|Anjali Mondal]], [[MRN|MRN-…]].
  Translate prose later — NEVER translate the TYPE token.

BAD → repair oscillation (fix A by breaking B):
  Round1 invents DATE to fit a table → Round2 English rewrite to clear invent →
  Round3 dialect_script_impurity → Round4 invent again.
GOOD:
  Fix ALL known issues together: correct ASCII tags + correct domain/persona +
  correct target script (or English pivot if this is the pre-translate stage).
  Never English-rewrite a finished Indic body just to repair tags.

Language SLA:
  hi/ta/te/bn/mr/… must stay fluent with tags — failures are our prompt/repair bug.
  brx/mni/sat (rare scripts) may soft-fail; do not collapse the whole run for them.

[[PATIENT_NAME|Karthik Raj]] is a 25-year-old male textile jobber from Kavundapalayam village, Erode district. Patient reports progressive cough for 3 months with occasional hemoptysis and 8kg weight loss over 2 months. No fever or night sweats reported. Family history negative for malignancies. Patient referred to [[HOSPITAL_NAME|Government Medical College Hospital Erode]] for further evaluation and management. [[ASHA_WORKER_NAME|Lakshmi Devi]] informed about patient's condition and follow-up required.

[[VOTER_ID|TN234567890]] | Voter identification
